chooseResponse.q: What time is the study group meeting?

conversation.transcript:
Student: Hey, did you hear about the study group for our biology class?
Advisor: Yes, I did! It's scheduled for tomorrow at 5 PM in the library.
Student: Great! I was worried it might be canceled.
Advisor: Not at all. The professor wants everyone to attend and discuss the upcoming exam.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the study group?
questions.explain: 這個學習小組的目的是為了準備考試。

questions.q: Where will the meeting take place?

announcement.transcript: Attention students! The campus bookstore will be holding a sale on textbooks this weekend. Everything will be 20% off, so be sure to stop by and stock up on your reading materials. Sale starts Friday and ends Sunday. Don't miss out!

questions.q: What is being sold at a discount?
questions.explain: 這次打折的商品是教科書。
這次打折活動從星期五開始。

talk.transcript: Today, I want to discuss the concept of renewable energy. Renewable energy refers to power generated from natural resources that are constantly replenished, such as sunlight, wind, rain, tides, and geothermal heat. Unlike fossil fuels, which are finite and contribute to environmental pollution, renewable energy sources offer a sustainable alternative. For instance, solar panels harness sunlight to produce electricity, while wind turbines use wind to generate power. This shift to renewable energy is crucial for reducing carbon emissions and combating climate change. By investing in renewable technologies, we can create a cleaner, healthier planet for future generations.

questions.explain: 可再生能源是來自自然資源的能源。

questions.q: What is one source of renewable energy mentioned?
questions.explain: 提到的一種可再生能源是風能。

options: It is cheaper than fossil fuels.
questions.explain: 可再生能源的重要性在於它有助於減少碳排放。

questions.q: What does the speaker suggest about future generations?
options: They will not have energy issues.
They will benefit from clean energy.
They will rely on fossil fuels.
questions.explain: 發言者建議未來的世代將受益於清潔能源。
